Description: Verify V4L2 video device can be opened and closed properly
Test Steps: Open device|Verify file descriptor valid|Close device
Expected Result: Device opens without errors|File descriptor >= 0|Device closes cleanly
Comments: Core functionality for all V4L2 operations

Test Steps: Call VIDIOC_QUERYCAP ioctl|Verify driver name not empty|Verify card name not empty|Check V4L2_CAP_VIDEO_CAPTURE flag
Expected Result: ioctl returns 0|Driver and card names populated|Video capture capability present

Description: Enumerate supported pixel formats using VIDIOC_ENUM_FMT
Test Steps: Set type to V4L2_BUF_TYPE_VIDEO_CAPTURE|Loop VIDIOC_ENUM_FMT with increasing index|Print format descriptions and fourcc codes|Count formats
Expected Result: At least one format enumerated|Each format has description and fourcc|Enumeration terminates gracefully
Comments: Typically expects Y16 or similar depth formats

Description: Get and set video format using VIDIOC_G_FMT and VIDIOC_S_FMT
Test Steps: Call VIDIOC_G_FMT to get current format|Verify width > 0 and height > 0|Verify sizeimage > 0|Call VIDIOC_S_FMT with same format
Expected Result: VIDIOC_G_FMT returns valid dimensions|Width/height match sensor specs (1024x1024 or 512x512)|VIDIOC_S_FMT succeeds
Comments: Format must match frame mode configuration

Description: Allocate and map V4L2 buffers using REQBUFS and MMAP
Test Steps: Call VIDIOC_REQBUFS for 4 MMAP buffers|Verify count >= 1|Query first buffer with VIDIOC_QUERYBUF|Map buffer with mmap()|Verify mapping != MAP_FAILED|Unmap buffer
Expected Result: REQBUFS allocates at least 1 buffer|QUERYBUF returns valid length and offset|mmap succeeds|munmap succeeds
Comments: Buffer count may be adjusted by driver

Description: Enumerate supported frame sizes using VIDIOC_ENUM_FRAMESIZES
Preconditions: Device opened successfully|At least one format available
Test Steps: Get first format fourcc|Loop VIDIOC_ENUM_FRAMESIZES with increasing index|Handle discrete or stepwise types|Print frame sizes
Expected Result: At least one frame size enumerated|Sizes match sensor capabilities (MP: 1024x1024; QMP: 512x512)|Enumeration terminates gracefully
Comments: Frame sizes depend on ISP firmware and mode

Test Steps: Set id to V4L2_CTRL_FLAG_NEXT_CTRL|Loop VIDIOC_QUERYCTRL|Skip disabled controls|Print control names and IDs
Expected Result: Controls enumerated without errors|Each control has name and ID|No disabled controls returned
Comments: Some devices may have no controls

Description: Start and stop streaming using VIDIOC_STREAMON/STREAMOFF
Preconditions: Device opened successfully|Buffers allocated and queued
Test Steps: Allocate 4 buffers with REQBUFS|Queue all buffers with VIDIOC_QBUF|Call VIDIOC_STREAMON|Verify success|Call VIDIOC_STREAMOFF|Verify success
Expected Result: STREAMON succeeds|STREAMOFF succeeds|No errors during start/stop cycle

Description: List formats with extended information using v4l2-ctl
Expected Result: Command executes successfully|Format information displayed|Frame sizes and intervals shown

Description: List supported pixel formats using v4l2-ctl
Get current video format using v4l2-ctl
Expected Result: Command executes successfully|Width/Height shown|Pixel format displayed

Description: List all V4L2 devices using v4l2-ctl
Expected Result: Command executes successfully|Device list shown|At least one device found

Description: Show all device information using v4l2-ctl
Expected Result: Command executes successfully|Driver info shown|Capabilities listed
Command executes successfully|Control list shown (may be empty)

Description: List subdev media bus codes using v4l2-ctl
Test Steps: Execute v4l2-ctl --list-subdev-mbus-codes|Capture output|Verify media bus formats
Expected Result: Command executes successfully|Media bus codes listed

Description: List subdev controls with details using v4l2-ctl
Test Steps: Execute v4l2-ctl -d /dev/v4l-subdev1 -L|Capture output|Display detailed control info
Expected Result: Command executes successfully|Detailed control info shown (may be empty)

Description: Print media topology in DOT format using media-ctl
Expected Result: Command executes successfully|DOT format output generated|Contains digraph structure
Comments: Output can be visualized with GraphViz

Expected Result: Command executes successfully|Entity information shown|Pad and link details displayed
